001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassArrayFormat.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassNameFormat.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
025
026import java.lang.annotation.*;
027import java.lang.reflect.*;
028import java.util.*;
029import java.util.function.*;
030import java.util.stream.*;
031
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
035
036/**
037 * Lightweight utility class for introspecting information about a Java field.
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * This class provides a convenient wrapper around {@link Field} that extends the standard Java reflection
041 * API with additional functionality for field introspection, annotation handling, and value access.
042 * It extends {@link AccessibleInfo} to provide {@link AccessibleObject} functionality for accessing
043 * private fields.
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
046 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
047 *    <li>Field introspection - access field metadata, type, modifiers
048 *    <li>Annotation support - get annotations declared on the field
049 *    <li>Value access - get and set field values with type safety
050 *    <li>Accessibility control - make private fields accessible
051 *    <li>Thread-safe - instances are immutable and safe for concurrent access
052 * </ul>
053 *
054 * <h5 class='section'>Use Cases:</h5>
055 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
056 *    <li>Introspecting field metadata for code generation or analysis
057 *    <li>Accessing field values in beans or data objects
058 *    <li>Finding annotations on fields
059 *    <li>Working with field types and modifiers
060 *    <li>Building frameworks that need to analyze or manipulate field values
061 * </ul>
062 *
063 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
064 * <p class='bjava'>
065 *    <jc>// Get FieldInfo from a class</jc>
066 *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
067 *    FieldInfo <jv>field</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.getField(<js>"myField"</js>);
068 *
069 *    <jc>// Get field type</jc>
070 *    ClassInfo <jv>type</jv> = <jv>field</jv>.getType();
071 *
072 *    <jc>// Get annotations</jc>
073 *    List&lt;AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt;&gt; <jv>annotations</jv> =
074 *       <jv>field</jv>.getAnnotations(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>).toList();
075 *
076 *    <jc>// Access field value</jc>
077 *    MyClass <jv>obj</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyClass();
078 *    <jv>field</jv>.accessible();  <jc>// Make accessible if private</jc>
079 *    Object <jv>value</jv> = <jv>field</jv>.get(<jv>obj</jv>);
080 * </p>
081 *
082 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
083 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ClassInfo} - Class introspection
084 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MethodInfo} - Method introspection
085 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ConstructorInfo} - Constructor introspection
086 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsReflection">Reflection Package</a>
087 * </ul>
088 */
089@SuppressWarnings({
090   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., CONST_value)
091   "java:S3011"  // Reflection access needed for field introspection
092})
093public class FieldInfo extends AccessibleInfo implements Comparable<FieldInfo>, Annotatable {
094
095   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
096   private static final String ARG_declaringClass = "declaringClass";
097   private static final String ARG_inner = "inner";
098
099   /**
100    * Creates a FieldInfo wrapper for the specified field.
101    *
102    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
103    * <p class='bjava'>
104    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
105    *    Field <jv>f</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getField(<js>"myField"</js>);
106    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = FieldInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>ci</jv>, <jv>f</jv>);
107    * </p>
108    *
109    * @param declaringClass The ClassInfo for the class that declares this field. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
110    * @param inner The field being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
111    * @return A new FieldInfo object wrapping the field.
112    */
113   public static FieldInfo of(ClassInfo declaringClass, Field inner) {
114      assertArgNotNull(ARG_declaringClass, declaringClass);
115      return declaringClass.getField(inner);
116   }
117
118   /**
119    * Creates a FieldInfo wrapper for the specified field.
120    *
121    * <p>
122    * This convenience method automatically determines the declaring class from the field.
123    *
124    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
125    * <p class='bjava'>
126    *    Field <jv>f</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getField(<js>"myField"</js>);
127    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = FieldInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>f</jv>);
128    * </p>
129    *
130    * @param inner The field being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
131    * @return A new FieldInfo object wrapping the field.
132    */
133   public static FieldInfo of(Field inner) {
134      assertArgNotNull(ARG_inner, inner);
135      return ClassInfo.of(inner.getDeclaringClass()).getField(inner);
136   }
137
138   private final Field inner;
139   private final ClassInfo declaringClass;
140   private final Supplier<ClassInfo> type;
141   private final Supplier<List<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>>> annotations;  // All annotations declared directly on this field.
142   private final Supplier<String> nameFull;  // Fully qualified field name (declaring-class.field-name).
143   private final Supplier<String> toString;  // String representation with modifiers, type, and full name.
144
145   /**
146    * Constructor.
147    *
148    * <p>
149    * Creates a new FieldInfo wrapper for the specified field. This constructor is protected
150    * and should not be called directly. Use the static factory methods {@link #of(Field)} or
151    * obtain FieldInfo instances from {@link ClassInfo#getField(Field)}.
152    *
153    * @param declaringClass The ClassInfo for the class that declares this field.
154    * @param inner The field being wrapped.
155    */
156   protected FieldInfo(ClassInfo declaringClass, Field inner) {
157      super(inner, inner.getModifiers());
158      assertArgNotNull(ARG_inner, inner);
159      this.declaringClass = declaringClass;
160      this.inner = inner;
161      this.type = memoize(() -> ClassInfo.of(inner.getType(), inner.getGenericType()));
162      this.annotations = memoize(() -> stream(inner.getAnnotations()).flatMap(AnnotationUtils::streamRepeated).map(a -> ai(this, a)).toList());
163      this.nameFull = memoize(this::findNameFull);
164      this.toString = memoize(this::findToString);
165   }
166
167   /**
168    * Attempts to call <code>x.setAccessible(<jk>true</jk>)</code> and quietly ignores security exceptions.
169    *
170    * @return This object.
171    */
172   public FieldInfo accessible() {
173      setAccessible();
174      return this;
175   }
176
177   @Override
178   public int compareTo(FieldInfo o) {
179      return cmp(getName(), o.getName());
180   }
181
182   /**
183    * Returns the field value on the specified object.
184    *
185    * <p>
186    * If the underlying field is static, then the specified <c>o</c> argument is ignored. It may be <jk>null</jk>.
187    *
188    * @param <T> The object type to retrieve.
189    * @param o The object containing the field.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for static fields.
190    * @return The field value.
191    * @throws BeanRuntimeException Field was not accessible or field does not belong to object.
192    */
193   @SuppressWarnings({
194      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to T for field retrieval
195   })
196   public <T> T get(Object o) throws BeanRuntimeException {
197      return safe(() -> {
198         inner.setAccessible(true);
199         return (T)inner.get(o);
200      }, e -> bex(e));
201   }
202
203   @Override /* Annotatable */
204   public AnnotatableType getAnnotatableType() { return AnnotatableType.FIELD_TYPE; }
205
206   /**
207    * Returns an {@link AnnotatedType} object that represents the use of a type to specify the declared type of the field.
208    *
209    * <p>
210    * Same as calling {@link Field#getAnnotatedType()}.
211    *
212    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
213    * <p class='bjava'>
214    *    <jc>// Get annotated type: @NotNull String name</jc>
215    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>).getField(<js>"name"</js>);
216    *    AnnotatedType <jv>aType</jv> = <jv>fi</jv>.getAnnotatedType();
217    *    <jc>// Check for @NotNull on the type</jc>
218    * </p>
219    *
220    * @return An {@link AnnotatedType} object representing the declared type.
221    * @see Field#getAnnotatedType()
222    */
223   public AnnotatedType getAnnotatedType() { return inner.getAnnotatedType(); }
224
225   /**
226    * Returns all annotations declared on this field.
227    *
228    * <p>
229    * <b>Note on Repeatable Annotations:</b>
230    * Repeatable annotations (those marked with {@link java.lang.annotation.Repeatable @Repeatable}) are automatically
231    * expanded into their individual annotation instances. For example, if a field has multiple {@code @Marshalled} annotations,
232    * this method returns each {@code @Marshalled} annotation separately, rather than the container annotation.
233    *
234    * @return
235    *    An unmodifiable list of all annotations declared on this field.
236    *    <br>Repeatable annotations are expanded into individual instances.
237    */
238   public List<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>> getAnnotations() { return annotations.get(); }
239
240   /**
241    * Returns all annotations of the specified type declared on this field.
242    *
243    * @param <A> The annotation type.
244    * @param type The annotation type.
245    * @return A stream of all matching annotations.
246    */
247   @SuppressWarnings({
248      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for annotation stream
249   })
250   public <A extends Annotation> Stream<AnnotationInfo<A>> getAnnotations(Class<A> type) {
251      return annotations.get().stream().filter(x -> type.isInstance(x.inner())).map(x -> (AnnotationInfo<A>)x);
252   }
253
254   /**
255    * Returns metadata about the declaring class.
256    *
257    * @return Metadata about the declaring class.
258    */
259   public ClassInfo getDeclaringClass() { return declaringClass; }
260
261   /**
262    * Returns the type of this field.
263    *
264    * @return The type of this field.
265    */
266   public ClassInfo getFieldType() { return type.get(); }
267
268   /**
269    * Returns the full name of this field.
270    *
271    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
272    * <ul>
273    *    <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass.myField"</js> - Method.
274    * </ul>
275    *
276    * @return The underlying executable name.
277    */
278   public String getNameFull() { return nameFull.get(); }
279
280   @Override /* Annotatable */
281   public String getLabel() { return getDeclaringClass().getNameSimple() + "." + getName(); }
282
283   /**
284    * Returns the name of this field.
285    *
286    * @return The name of this field.
287    */
288   public String getName() { return inner.getName(); }
289
290   /**
291    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified annotation is present.
292    *
293    * @param <A> The annotation type to look for.
294    * @param type The annotation to look for.
295    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified annotation is present.
296    */
297   public <A extends Annotation> boolean hasAnnotation(Class<A> type) {
298      return getAnnotations(type).findAny().isPresent();
299   }
300
301   /**
302    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the field has the specified name.
303    *
304    * @param name The name to compare against.
305    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the field has the specified name.
306    */
307   public boolean hasName(String name) {
308      return inner.getName().equals(name);
309   }
310
311   /**
312    * Returns the wrapped field.
313    *
314    * @return The wrapped field.
315    */
316   public Field inner() {
317      return inner;
318   }
319
320   /**
321    * Compares this FieldInfo with the specified object for equality.
322    *
323    * <p>
324    * Two FieldInfo objects are considered equal if they wrap the same underlying {@link Field} object.
325    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Field#equals(Object)} method.
326    *
327    * <p>
328    * This method makes FieldInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
329    * and {@link HashSet}.
330    *
331    * @param obj The object to compare with.
332    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are equal, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
333    */
334   @Override
335   public boolean equals(Object obj) {
336      return obj instanceof FieldInfo other && eq(this, other, (x, y) -> eq(x.inner, y.inner));
337   }
338
339   /**
340    * Returns a hash code value for this FieldInfo.
341    *
342    * <p>
343    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Field#hashCode()} method.
344    *
345    * <p>
346    * This method makes FieldInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
347    * and {@link HashSet}.
348    *
349    * @return A hash code value for this FieldInfo.
350    */
351   @Override
352   public int hashCode() {
353      return inner.hashCode();
354   }
355
356   /**
357    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if all specified flags are applicable to this field.
358    *
359    * @param flag The flag to test for.
360    * @return <jk>true</jk> if all specified flags are applicable to this field.
361    */
362   @Override
363   public boolean is(ElementFlag flag) {
364      return switch (flag) {
365         case DEPRECATED -> isDeprecated();
366         case NOT_DEPRECATED -> isNotDeprecated();
367         case ENUM_CONSTANT -> isEnumConstant();
368         case NOT_ENUM_CONSTANT -> ! isEnumConstant();
369         case SYNTHETIC -> isSynthetic();
370         case NOT_SYNTHETIC -> ! isSynthetic();  // HTT
371         default -> super.is(flag);
372      };
373   }
374
375   /**
376    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this field has the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
377    *
378    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this field has the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
379    */
380   public boolean isDeprecated() { return inner.isAnnotationPresent(Deprecated.class); }
381
382   /**
383    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this field represents an element of an enumerated type.
384    *
385    * <p>
386    * Same as calling {@link Field#isEnumConstant()}.
387    *
388    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
389    * <p class='bjava'>
390    *    <jc>// Check if field is an enum constant</jc>
391    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyEnum.<jk>class</jk>).getField(<js>"VALUE1"</js>);
392    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>fi</jv>.isEnumConstant()) {
393    *       <jc>// Handle enum constant</jc>
394    *    }
395    * </p>
396    *
397    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this field represents an enum constant.
398    * @see Field#isEnumConstant()
399    */
400   public boolean isEnumConstant() { return inner.isEnumConstant(); }
401
402   /**
403    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this field doesn't have the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
404    *
405    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this field doesn't have the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
406    */
407   public boolean isNotDeprecated() { return ! inner.isAnnotationPresent(Deprecated.class); }
408
409   /**
410    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this field is a synthetic field as defined by the Java Language Specification.
411    *
412    * <p>
413    * Same as calling {@link Field#isSynthetic()}.
414    *
415    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
416    * <p class='bjava'>
417    *    <jc>// Filter out compiler-generated fields</jc>
418    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ...;
419    *    <jk>if</jk> (! <jv>fi</jv>.isSynthetic()) {
420    *       <jc>// Process real field</jc>
421    *    }
422    * </p>
423    *
424    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this field is a synthetic field.
425    * @see Field#isSynthetic()
426    */
427   public boolean isSynthetic() { return inner.isSynthetic(); }
428
429   /**
430    * Identifies if the specified visibility matches this field.
431    *
432    * @param v The visibility to validate against.
433    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this visibility matches the modifier attribute of this field.
434    */
435   public boolean isVisible(Visibility v) {
436      return v.isVisible(inner);
437   }
438
439   /**
440    * Sets the field value on the specified object.
441    *
442    * <p>
443    * If the underlying field is static, then the specified <c>o</c> argument is ignored. It may be <jk>null</jk>.
444    *
445    * @param o The object containing the field.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for static fields.
446    * @param value The new field value.
447    * @throws BeanRuntimeException Field was not accessible or field does not belong to object.
448    */
449   public void set(Object o, Object value) throws BeanRuntimeException {
450      safe((Snippet)() -> {
451         inner.setAccessible(true);
452         inner.set(o, value);
453      }, e -> bex(e));
454   }
455
456   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
457   // Field-Specific Methods
458   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
459
460   /**
461    * Sets the field value on the specified object if the value is <jk>null</jk>.
462    *
463    * @param o The object containing the field.
464    * @param value The new field value.
465    * @throws BeanRuntimeException Field was not accessible or field does not belong to object.
466    */
467   public void setIfNull(Object o, Object value) {
468      Object v = get(o);
469      if (v == null)
470         set(o, value);
471   }
472
473   /**
474    * Returns a string describing this field, including its generic type.
475    *
476    * <p>
477    * Same as calling {@link Field#toGenericString()}.
478    *
479    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
480    * <p class='bjava'>
481    *    <jc>// Get generic string for: public static final List&lt;String&gt; VALUES</jc>
482    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>).getField(<js>"VALUES"</js>);
483    *    String <jv>str</jv> = <jv>fi</jv>.toGenericString();
484    *    <jc>// Returns: "public static final java.util.List&lt;java.lang.String&gt; com.example.MyClass.VALUES"</jc>
485    * </p>
486    *
487    * @return A string describing this field.
488    * @see Field#toGenericString()
489    */
490   public String toGenericString() {
491      return inner.toGenericString();
492   }
493
494   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
495   // Annotatable interface methods
496   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
497
498   /**
499    * Returns a detailed string representation of this field.
500    *
501    * <p>
502    * The returned string includes:
503    * <ul>
504    *    <li>Modifiers (public, private, protected, static, final, volatile, transient)
505    *    <li>Field type with generics (e.g., "List&lt;String&gt;")
506    *    <li>Fully qualified field name (declaring-class.field-name)
507    * </ul>
508    *
509    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
510    * <p class='bjava'>
511    *    <jc>// Simple field</jc>
512    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ...;
513    *    <jv>fi</jv>.toString();
514    *    <jc>// Returns: "public java.lang.String org.example.MyClass.name"</jc>
515    *
516    *    <jc>// Static final field</jc>
517    *    <jc>// Returns: "public static final int org.example.MyClass.MAX_VALUE"</jc>
518    *
519    *    <jc>// Generic field</jc>
520    *    <jc>// Returns: "private java.util.List&lt;java.lang.String&gt; org.example.MyClass.items"</jc>
521    *
522    *    <jc>// Volatile field</jc>
523    *    <jc>// Returns: "volatile boolean org.example.MyClass.flag"</jc>
524    * </p>
525    *
526    * @return A detailed string representation including modifiers, type, and full name.
527    */
528   @Override
529   public String toString() {
530      return toString.get();
531   }
532
533   private String findToString() {
534      var sb = new StringBuilder(256);
535
536      // Modifiers
537      var mods = Modifier.toString(getModifiers());
538      if (nn(mods) && ! mods.isEmpty()) {
539         sb.append(mods).append(" ");
540      }
541
542      // Field type (use generic type to show generics)
543      var genericType = inner.getGenericType();
544      ClassInfo.of(genericType).appendNameFormatted(sb, FULL, true, '$', BRACKETS);
545
546      // Fully qualified field name
547      sb.append(" ").append(getNameFull());
548
549      return sb.toString();
550   }
551
552   private String findNameFull() {
553      var sb = new StringBuilder(128);
554      var dc = declaringClass;
555      var pi = dc.getPackage();
556      if (nn(pi))
557         sb.append(pi.getName()).append('.');
558      // HTT - false branch (pi == null) is hard to test: some classloaders return Package objects
559      // even for default package classes, though Java API spec says it should return null
560      dc.appendNameFormatted(sb, SHORT, true, '$', BRACKETS);
561      sb.append('.').append(getName());
562      return sb.toString();
563   }
564
565   /**
566    * Resolves field value from the bean store and sets it on the specified bean instance.
567    *
568    * <p>
569    * This method resolves the field value using the same logic as parameter resolution,
570    * supporting single beans, {@code Optional}, arrays, {@code List}, {@code Set}, and {@code Map}.
571    * The field is made accessible before setting its value.
572    *
573    * <h5 class='section'>Field Resolution:</h5>
574    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
575    *    <li><b>Single beans</b> - Resolved using {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class)} or {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class, String)}.
576    *       Throws {@link ExecutableException} if not found.
577    *    <li><b>Optional beans</b> - Wrapped in <c>Optional</c>, or <c>Optional.empty()</c> if not found.
578    *       Never throws an exception.
579    *    <li><b>Arrays</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into an array (may be empty).
580    *       Never throws an exception.
581    *    <li><b>Lists</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>List</c> (may be empty).
582    *       Never throws an exception.
583    *    <li><b>Sets</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashSet</c> (may be empty).
584    *       Never throws an exception.
585    *    <li><b>Maps</b> - All beans of the value type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashMap</c> keyed by bean name (may be empty).
586    *       Unnamed beans use an empty string as the key.  Never throws an exception.
587    * </ul>
588    *
589    * @param <T> The bean type.
590    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve the field value from.
591    * @param bean The object instance containing the field.
592    * @return The same bean instance (for method chaining).
593    * @throws ExecutableException If a required field (non-Optional, non-collection) cannot be resolved from the bean store.
594    */
595   @SuppressWarnings({
596      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for dependency injection
597   })
598   public <T> T inject(BeanStore beanStore, T bean) {
599      accessible();
600      var fieldType = getFieldType();
601
602      // @Value sites resolve from Settings/VarResolver, not the bean store.
603      var annos = getAnnotations();
604      var valueExpr = ValueResolver.findValueExpression(annos);
605      if (valueExpr != null) {
606         ValueResolver.checkInjectConflict(annos, this.toString());
607         var unwrapped = fieldType.unwrap(Optional.class).inner();
608         // Pass the declared (generic) field type so @Value Supplier<String> autodetects
609         // Field type IS the opt-in signal for re-evaluating reads (Supplier<String> autodetect).
610         // BeanStore is forwarded so caller-scoped PropertySource beans (e.g. per-RestContext
611         // @Rest(config=...) Configs) participate in expression resolution alongside Settings.
612         var resolved = ValueResolver.resolve(valueExpr, unwrapped, inner.getGenericType(), this.toString(), beanStore);
613         if (fieldType.is(Optional.class)) {
614            // VarResolver substitutes "" for a missing key with no default. Collapse both to
615            // Optional.empty() so @Value("${maybe}") Optional<T> behaves the same as Spring's.
616            set(bean, (resolved == null || (resolved instanceof String s && s.isEmpty()))
617               ? opte()
618               : opt(resolved));
619         } else {
620            set(bean, resolved);
621         }
622         return bean;
623      }
624
625      // Find qualifier from @Named or @Qualifier annotation (same logic as ParameterInfo.getResolvedQualifier)
626      var beanQualifier = annos.stream()
627         .map(JsrSupport::qualifierValue)
628         .filter(Objects::nonNull)
629         .findFirst()
630         .orElse(null);
631
632      var ptUnwrapped = fieldType.unwrap(Optional.class);
633
634      // Handle collections and arrays
635      Object collectionValue = null;
636      if (ptUnwrapped.isInjectCollectionType()) {
637         // Extract element type from field type
638         Class<?> elementType = null;
639
640         if (ptUnwrapped.isArray()) {
641            elementType = ptUnwrapped.getComponentType().inner();
642         } else {
643            Type parameterizedType = fieldType.innerType();
644            var inner2 = opt(ptUnwrapped.inner()).orElse(Object.class);
645
646            if (eq(inner2, List.class) || eq(inner2, Set.class)) {
647               if (parameterizedType instanceof ParameterizedType pt2) {
648                  var typeArgs = pt2.getActualTypeArguments();
649                  if (typeArgs.length > 0 && typeArgs[0] instanceof Class<?> elementClass) {
650                     elementType = elementClass;
651                  }
652               }
653            } else if (eq(inner2, Map.class) && parameterizedType instanceof ParameterizedType pt2) {
654               var typeArgs = pt2.getActualTypeArguments();
655               if (typeArgs.length >= 2 && typeArgs[0] == String.class && typeArgs[1] instanceof Class<?> valueClass) {
656                  elementType = valueClass;
657               }
658            }
659         }
660
661         collectionValue = ReflectionUtils.resolveCollectionValue(elementType, beanStore, ptUnwrapped);
662      }
663
664      Object value;
665      if (nn(collectionValue)) {
666         value = fieldType.is(Optional.class) ? opt(collectionValue) : collectionValue;
667      } else {
668         // Handle single bean
669         var ptc = ptUnwrapped.inner();
670         var o2 = beanQualifier == null ? beanStore.getBean(ptc) : beanStore.getBean(ptc, beanQualifier);
671
672         if (fieldType.is(Optional.class)) {
673            value = o2;
674         } else if (o2.isPresent()) {
675            value = o2.get();
676         } else {
677            throw exex("Could not resolve value for field {0}", this);
678         }
679      }
680
681      set(bean, value);
682      return bean;
683   }
684}
